title: Table Saw Outfeed Table with Storage Cabinet
summary: Build a simple tablesaw outfeed table using pocket holes, 2×4’s , and plywood.
description: Build a simple tablesaw outfeed table using pocket holes, 2×4’s , and plywood. This whole build is made using the KREG Foreman and KREG Hardware Installation products.

steps.title: Material List
steps.body:
For this Project you will need the following
Materials - (12) 2" x 4" x 8' Dimensioned lumber - (2) 4' x 8' x 3/4" Plywood ( Grade choice is up to you) - Glue - Pocket Hole Screws - 1 1/4" 23g Pin Nails - (1) 4' x 8' x 3/4" Melamine - (2) Sets Drawer Slides - (2) Sets Door Hinges - (4) Door/ Drawer Pull Hardware

steps.title: TABLE BUILD -Break Down Stock
steps.body: - Begin breaking down your stock based on the height of your table saw and your flooring. Cut everything to rough length then final dimensions later - Mill down 2x4 stock to 1 1/4" x 2 1/2"

steps.title: Glue Up Legs
steps.body: - Glue up legs (Make same height of table saw and cut to final length after)

steps.title: Dimension Stretchers
steps.body: - Cut all stretchers to the length in the diagram

steps.title: Pocket Hole Stretchers
steps.body: - Pocket Hole Stretchers using KREG Foreman. - Adjust Foreman for a deeper pocket hole than 1"

steps.title: Cut Legs and Attach Stretchers
steps.body: - Cut table legs to final dimension ( add adjustable feet if floor is uneven) - Cut to 1" lower than table top height for addition of top thickness - Attach Stretchers using Pocket Hole Screws and glue - Use a 4" spacer block on the bottom for shelf height

steps.title: Add Cross Bracing
steps.body: - Add cross bracing in between long stretchers - Cross bracing dimensions are on a project basis, measure the distance in between stretchers and cut to length. - This can be any dimensioned stock or scrap wood in shop (I used old 2x3's cut to 41 1/2")

steps.title: Add Corner Gussets
steps.body: - Using left over scrap from stretchers, cut 45* corner braces to whichever length you desire - Add Pocket Holes and Attach with glue and screws

steps.title: CABINET BUILD - Break Down Ply
steps.body: - Break down sheet goods into dimensioned parts - I use a track saw and table saw for final dimensions - recommend cutting from 2 sheets but can be done with 1 and leftover shop scraps for face frame

steps.title: Pocket Hole Stretchers
steps.body:
- Pocket Hole stretchers. - Add one stretcher to the toe kick - add one stretcher to the top front (Flush with top of box) - add stretchers to back (top and bottom flush with back) - Cut 3" x 3" toe kick into sides with jigsaw
BOTTOM SHELF WILL SIT ON TOP OF STRETCHERS, BE SURE TO HAVE TOE KICK STRETCHER AND BOTTOM BACK STRETCHER ON CABINET BOTTOM WITH POCKET HOLES FACING IN

steps.title: Cut and Glue Face Frame
steps.body: - Using scrap hardwood or scrap plywood (or second sheet of plywood) cut and assemble face frame based on diagram.

steps.title: Insert Bottom
steps.body:
- Insert cabinet bottom on top of stretchers. - Apply glue and mark from top. - Nail bottom into place
NOTE: Measure box interior, minor changes may affect precut size of bottom

steps.title: Attach Divider
steps.body: - Attach center divider for drawer slides with pocket holes in back and screws from top down front stretcher

steps.title: Attach Face Frame
steps.body: - Attach Face Frame with 1 1/4" 23g pin nails and glue

steps.title: Cut Dados In Drawers
steps.body:
- Cut 1/2" Dado inset 1/4" from bottom of drawer sides and front - pocket hole drawer sides
NOTE: Cut drawer backs 1/2" to allow bottom panel to slide in and screw from back

steps.title: Assemble Drawers
steps.body:
- Assemble drawers with pocket holes on front and back - Face pocket holes out - Insert Bottom into dados
- In photo you can see pocket holes facing out, bottom panel is slid in and attached with 2 screws from the bottom into the drawer back (the one you cut 1/2" short)
NOTE : Drawer box dimensions may change depending on slides. Typical Ball Bearing slides need 1" of total clearance (1/2" each side) so make drawer box width 1" short of face frame width

steps.title: Attach Slides
steps.body: - Using a flat surface and fully assembles slide, attach to drawer box.

steps.title: Attach Slides To Cabinet
steps.body:
- Using KREG Drawer Slide Jig attach slides to cabinet box. - Insert drawer box, adjust accordingly
NOTE: You may need to shim out the interior of the cabinet box so the drawer slides are flush with the inside of the face frame. use scraps or whatever is lying around, glue and brads work

steps.title: Drill and Mount Door Hardware
steps.body: - Using KREG concealed hinge jig, mount hardware to doors. - Install doors in cabinet box

steps.title: Install Pulls and Hardware
steps.body: - Using KREG Cabinet Hardware Jig , Mount drawer and door handles

steps.title: Add Top
steps.body: - Cut Melamine top to size and attach from bottom. - Should span from the cabinet all the way to table saw - If cut tall, use router to create mitre gauge slots - Round over edges on melamine and finish with 220 grit sandpaper
